name: Functor
def: A type supporting `map`: applying a function inside a context while preserving its structure.
problem: Transform wrapped values (lists, options, futures) without unwrapping or changing their shape.
mechanism: Defines `map(f)` obeying identity (map id = id) and composition (map (g∘f) = map g ∘ map f).
limits: Without HKTs the abstraction can’t be expressed generically in mainstream languages
failures: map implementations that violate composition (side effects)
whenNot: When you only ever have one concrete container
myths: "Functor = monad" — every monad is a functor, not vice versa
